name: Acrivon Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Acrivon Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company discovering and developing precision medicines using its proprietary AP3 platform, initially focused on oncology. The company's pipeline includes ACR-368, a Phase 2b CHK1/2 inhibitor in-licensed from Lilly, and ACR-2316, an internally discovered dual WEE1/PKMYT1 inhibitor in Phase 1. The company has no approved products and no revenue from drug sales.

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company discovering and developing precision medicines utilizing our proprietary Generative Phosphoproteomics Acrivon Predictive Precision Proteomics, or AP3, platform.

catalysts.event: Initiation of arm 4 (serous EC all-comer ACR-368 monotherapy) in first half of 2026
catalysts.sentence: a fourth arm, designed with registrational intent, is planned for initiation in the first half of 2026, also in in serous EC, biomarker-unselected ( all-comer ) subjects with 2 prior lines of therapy, for treatment with ACR-368 monotherapy, without ULDG.

catalysts.event: Planned IND filing for ACR-6840 in fourth quarter 2026
catalysts.sentence: The agent is being advanced in IND enabling studies for planned IND filing in the fourth quarter 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Only 9% of all patients with cancer have tumors with genetic profiles that make them eligible for an available precision oncology medicine.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that only 9% of all patients with cancer have tumors with genetic profiles that make them eligible for an available precision oncology medicine, so-called genetically-defined cancers, and only 5% of all patients with cancer are likely to benefit from available therapies.

tamClaims.claim: Only 5% of all patients with cancer are likely to benefit from available therapies.
tamClaims.sentence: It is estimated that only 9% of all patients with cancer have tumors with genetic profiles that make them eligible for an available precision oncology medicine, so-called genetically-defined cancers, and only 5% of all patients with cancer are likely to benefit from available therapies.
